metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:sigkill (also exit_clean/restart/wait_turn unported)

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes — vibe exits cleanly and can be killed like any process.
daemon_capability: full — the process-exit lifecycle is wired for vibe.
driver_capability: gap:sigkill — the scaffolded driver has not ported sigkill (nor exit_clean / restart / wait_turn); the recipe needs all four.

## Reasoning

Session end has three variants (clean exit, SIGKILL mid-idle, SIGKILL mid-turn). All three are teardown paths on a normal OS process, and the defining question is whether irrlicht observes the disappearance and settles the session — not whether the agent has a special feature.

**agent = yes.** Vibe is a Textual TUI. Ctrl+D quits (confirming twice within ~1s, or once when `ask_confirmation_on_exit=false`), and there is an explicit `/exit` slash command (aliases `/exit`,`exit`,`quit`,`:q`,`:quit`). SIGKILL is OS-level and agent-agnostic. So all three teardown variants are exercisable against a live vibe process.

**daemon = full.** The vibe adapter declares `Process.Match = CommandPattern{processCmdRegex}` and `Process.PIDForSession = DiscoverPID` (agent.go), and `DiscoverPID` binds the vibe PID by cwd+cmdline (pid.go). Process-exit handling is generic and adapter-agnostic: `PIDManager.HandleProcessExit` (core/application/services/pid_manager.go:303) records `lifecycle.KindProcessExited` and deletes the session (with children) the moment the registered PID disappears — the same mechanism that gives claudecode's session-end cell daemon=full. The working→ready settle on exit and the ready→removed TTL are shared daemon behavior in session_detector_activity / the liveness sweep, not adapter code, so vibe inherits them. The session-end signal is therefore process_exited (vibe keeps transcripts under ~/.vibe/logs/session/ — it does not delete messages.jsonl on exit — so file-removal is NOT the teardown signal; process-exit is). Verified against a live session dir (session_20260706_101952_48e134e3) holding messages.jsonl + meta.json.

**driver = gap:sigkill.** The templated recipe (from claudecode's 3-variant sibling) needs `wait_turn`, `exit_clean`, `restart`, and `sigkill`. The scaffolded driver-interactive.sh has every arm PRESENT but stubbed; its `DRIVE_ELICITS` lists only `send slash sleep`, so recipe-lint flags all four as semantic gaps before recording. sigkill is the load-bearing miss (2 of 3 variants); exit_clean/restart/wait_turn are also unported. `record` must port these seams (from the codex/gemini slot-model drivers) before it can drive this cell — the cell stays a real cell with a real recipe and spec.

## What is provisional

daemon=full is derived from the generic process-exit path plus the claudecode precedent, not from a vibe recording — kept at confidence 0.8. The session-end signal is OS process exit (not agent transcript emission), so the usual emission caveat is weak here; a live drive still promotes it from provisional to settled.
caveats: Driver: exit_clean for vibe is Ctrl+D with a confirmation prompt (press twice within ~1s, or set ask_confirmation_on_exit=false), or the `/exit` slash command — the exit_clean seam port must account for the confirm dialog. `/exit` is deliverable via the already-implemented `slash` step but still triggers the same confirm path.

Variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn): the post-crash working→ready sweep transition may not land inside the recording's tail window — shared daemon behavior, same accommodation as the claudecode sibling (anchor v3_exit to v3_turn_start, do not require an explicit post-crash ready phase).
The JSONL carries no per-event timestamps; live tailing stamps time.Now (ParseTimestamp), so ordering within a live recording is fine — not a session-end blocker.

Per-turn cost is not emitted (the sidecar retains only last-turn tokens) and mistral models are absent from core/pkg/capacity, so no cost/utilization observations are asserted; session-end is a lifecycle scenario and (like the claudecode sibling) asserts no observation vector.

sources.note: Process.Match = CommandPattern{processCmdRegex} + PIDForSession = DiscoverPID — the wiring that lets the daemon detect vibe process exit.
HandleProcessExit (line 303) records KindProcessExited and deletes the session on PID disappearance — generic, adapter-agnostic; drives session-end for any adapter that registers a PID.
Clean-exit surface: aliases frozenset(["/exit","exit","quit",":q",":quit"]); textual_ui/app.py binds ctrl+d to quit; skills/builtins/vibe.py documents Ctrl+D-to-quit with confirmation.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn/exit_clean/restart/sigkill arms are stubbed not_implemented — the recipe's teardown primitives are unported (driver gap).

Live session dir holds messages.jsonl + meta.json (active_model=mistral-medium-3.5, parent_session_id=null) — transcript persists after exit, confirming process-exit (not file-removal) is the session-end signal.

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (v2.19.0) and authenticated
irrlichd is recording (run-cell.sh isolated mode, or --attach against a --record daemon)
driver-interactive.sh has ported the wait_turn, exit_clean, restart and sigkill seams (currently stubbed) — see driver gap
setup: Vibe mints its own session id (session_<timestamp>_<hash>) per launch; the driver resolves it from ~/.vibe/logs/session and keys the daemon on the session dir
Each restart step launches a fresh vibe process → a new session dir/id

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok
Write a 200 word essay about the history of coffee.

verify: events.jsonl contains 3 distinct vibe session_ids (one per variant), each with its process_exited
variant 1 (clean exit): the session returns to ready right after its turn completes, immediately preceding its process_exited
variant 2 (SIGKILL idle): the session ends in ready when sigkill fires; no state_transition to working after process_exited
variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn): the session is working when sigkill fires; a working→ready sweep may or may not appear in the 8s tail — accept either shape
the state band shows three colored arcs separated by grey gaps where no session is alive between variants
